title: Change sudo examples to use $ instead of $
body:
sudo is not required when running as root, and # indicates
that you are already root.  $ indicates you are not root.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: LGTM, but probably want to let @baude weigh in.
I am not going to fight with this. Error is unrelated, this is a docs only change. Will merge manually.

The change LGTM. I had a gist from the Docker man page/tutorial days that I wanted to make into a podman tutorial. I will take a look and see where it fits in.  But most of it would be around -v  options.

https://gist.github.com/ipbabble/c7f231d6ebf596e61cd6
